title: Kosteletzkya smilacifolia
taxonomy: Kosteletzkya smilacifolia is classified as a species within the Malvaceae family, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as mallow or hibiscus family. It is further classified as part of the genus Kosteletzkya, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. Kosteletzkya smilacifolia is a type of flowering plant that is native to West Africa, and it is often found growing in wetlands and other areas with high water tables. The classification of Kosteletzkya smilacifolia is also reflected in its botanical name, which is based on the Greek words 'kostele' meaning 'small' and 'smilacifolia' meaning 'false-smilax'. Overall, the classification of Kosteletzkya smilacifolia provides important information about its relationships to other plants and its evolutionary history.
uses: Kosteletzkya smilacifolia, a plant species commonly found in tropical regions, has been utilized in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits.
